deterministic_only.name: deterministic_only
deterministic_only.description: Deterministic search, ranking, claim extraction, council, and synthesis. No local LLM.

llm_synthesis_only.name: llm_synthesis_only
llm_synthesis_only.description: Deterministic evidence pipeline with local LLM used only for final synthesis.

llm_claims_and_synthesis.name: llm_claims_and_synthesis
llm_claims_and_synthesis.description: Local LLM extracts claims and synthesizes summary; deterministic safety still applies.

full_llm_council.name: full_llm_council
full_llm_council.description: Local LLM assists claim extraction, conflict checking, safety review, final review, and synthesis. Deterministic fallbacks remain active.

hybrid_recommended.name: hybrid_recommended
hybrid_recommended.description: Deterministic privacy, source validation, recency, and safety with local LLM for claim extraction and synthesis only. Recommended production tradeoff.

deterministic_only.question: latest guideline for community acquired pneumonia antibiotics in adults

result.clinical_question: latest guideline for community acquired pneumonia antibiotics in adults
result.sanitized_query: latest guideline for community acquired pneumonia antibiotics in adults guideline recommendation updated WHO CDC NICE NIH PubMed
result.summary: For physician review, guideline-level sources suggest the following preliminary evidence for: latest guideline for community acquired pneumonia antibiotics in adults. NICE guideline recommendations describe diagnosis, severity assessment, antimicrobial prescribing, and management of pneumonia in adults. Recommendations require clinician judgment. CDC clinical guidance and vaccination recommendations are updated for public-health practice. Key reviewed sources: NICE pneumonia in adults guideline (nice.org.uk, updated/published 2023-10-31); CDC clinical guidance for respiratory infections (cdc.gov, updated/published 2024-03-01); FDA drug safety communications (fda.gov, updated/published 2024-01-15). This is not a final diagnosis or direct patient treatment instruction; it requires clinician judgment.

key_findings.claim: NICE guideline recommendations describe diagnosis, severity assessment, antimicrobial prescribing, and management of pneumonia in adults.
Recommendations require clinician judgment.
CDC clinical guidance and vaccination recommendations are updated for public-health practice.
Clinicians should consider risk groups, warnings, contraindications, and current surveillance.
FDA drug safety communications provide warnings, contraindications, and updated safety information for medicines.
Clinicians should review the full label before treatment decisions.
Guideline recommendations for community-acquired pneumonia in adults should be interpreted with clinical judgment.
Recommendations discuss empiric antibiotic treatment, diagnostic assessment, and management decisions for physician review.

result.summary: Current guideline management of atrial fibrillation anticoagulation is complex and requires careful consideration of risk groups, warnings, contraindications, and surveillance. Clinicians should review the full label before treatment decisions to ensure informed treatment choices. However, uncertainty remains regarding optimal management strategies, particularly in individual patient contexts. The following guidelines provide evidence-based recommendations: CDC clinical guidance for respiratory infections, NICE pneumonia in adults guideline, CDC adult immunization schedule, FDA drug safety communications, and IDSA community-acquired pneumonia guideline. Physician review is essential to integrate these findings into individual patient care plans.
